name: Puebla de Maestre and Embalse del Pintado Loop
name_original: Rodea el Embalse del Pintado y Cazalla de la Sierra desde Puebla de Maestre — circular por el Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla
description: This difficult 69.3-mile road cycling route circles the Embalse del Pintado and passes Cazalla de la Sierra in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla N
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Puebla Del Maestre traverse diverse landscapes characterized by irregular and rugged terrain, featuring a combination of mountains, forests, and valleys. Cyclists encounter distinctive dry stone landscapes, ancient olive groves, and carob trees, often cultivated on terraced land. The region's routes frequently include significant elevation gains, offering panoramic views of the surrounding area. Best road cycling routes around Puebla Del Maestre The most popular road cycling route is Puebla de Maestre and Embalse del Pintado Loop, a 69.3 miles (111.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Santa Olalla Castle – Castillo del Real de la Jara loop from Monesterio, a difficult 55.5 miles (89.4 km) path. This route offers a challenging ride through the region's hilly landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Section through the Extremaduran dehesa – Section approaching Monasterio loop from Monesterio, a 54.4 miles (87.6 km) trail leading through the Extremaduran dehesa, often completed in about 4 hours 55 minutes. Road cycling around Puebla Del Maestre is defined by rugged land, extensive forests, and deep ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes present significant climbing challenges. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puebla Del Maestre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Puebla Del Maestre, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. Most of these routes are categorized as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter while road cycling in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: The region offers diverse and rugged landscapes, including mountains, forests, and valleys. You'll cycle through distinctive dry stone landscapes, ancient olive groves, and carob trees, often cultivated on terraced land. Routes like the 'Barranc Fondo' take you into lush ravines, while 'La Perdiguera' offers extensive panoramic views from high points.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: Road cycling in Puebla Del Maestre is characterized by challenging terrain. Out of the available routes, the vast majority are classified as difficult, featuring substantial elevation gains and demanding climbs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992819975/puebla-de-maestre-and-embalse-del-pintado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Maestre and Embalse del Pintado Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers over 111 km with more than 1600 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Puebla Del Maestre offers several long-distance road cycling routes. Many routes exceed 80 kilometers, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992819975/puebla-de-maestre-and-embalse-del-pintado-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puebla de Maestre and Embalse del Pintado Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 111.6 km long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo del Real de la Jara – Santa Olalla Castle loop from Llerena&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 111.7 km.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I visit along the road cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore several points of interest. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3518848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montemolín&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also pass through the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4356568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Landscape&lt;/a&gt; and are near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1137424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy about the routes in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: Road cyclists frequently appreciate the tranquil environment and the diverse scenery, which includes rugged mountains, forests, and valleys. The low agricultural traffic and generally good road surfaces also contribute to a peaceful and enjoyable riding experience, allowing for immersion in the natural beauty and historical elements of the region.

Q: Are the road surfaces suitable for road cycling in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Puebla Del Maestre benefit from good road surfaces. This, combined with a low density of agricultural traffic, makes for peaceful and enjoyable rides, allowing cyclists to focus on the scenery and the challenge of the terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for road cycling, offering milder temperatures compared to the heat of summer. These seasons provide comfortable conditions to tackle the significant elevation gains and enjoy the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Puebla Del Maestre are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38208913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Olalla Castle – Castillo del Real de la Jara loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section through the Extremaduran dehesa – Section approaching Monasterio loop from Monesterio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: The road cycling routes around Puebla Del Maestre are predominantly characterized by difficult terrain and significant elevation gains, making them more suitable for experienced cyclists. While specific family-friendly routes are not highlighted, those seeking easier rides might need to look for shorter, flatter sections or consider other cycling disciplines.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on road cycling routes in Puebla Del Maestre?
A: Road cycling routes in Puebla Del Maestre are known for their substantial elevation gains. Many difficult routes feature climbs well over 1000 meters. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Section approaching Monasterio – Section through the Extremaduran dehesa loop from Llerena&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1150 meters of ascent.
tips: Road that passes through oak forests for what are known as Dehesas Estremeñas. In the background, you can see the mountain slopes of the Sierras de las Cachoneras and the Sierra de la Traviesa.
The asphalt is not the best (at least that was the case in 2013), but it is an extremely low-traffic road through the national park. Very nice bike tour.
Cazalla de la Sierra is a beautiful town with a lot of history. Worth a visit.
This is a new stretch of road that is currently asphalted and that joins the road that comes from the town of Cazalla de la Sierra with the one that comes from the center of Puebla del Maestre.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a "difficult" road ride, requiring very good fitness. While primarily for road cycling, be prepared for varied surfaces that might include short, unpaved or less-than-perfect tarmac segments, especially within the natural park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the mountainous landscape of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, characterized by gentle relief. Expect diverse scenery including extensive dehesa landscapes with holm oaks and cork oaks, particularly scenic on the road to the Embalse del Pintado from Cazalla de la Sierra. You'll also enjoy picturesque views of the Embalse del Pintado itself and pass through charming rural villages.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts from Puebla de Maestre. As a common starting point for outdoor activities, you should find suitable parking within or near the village.

faqs.question: What are some notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning views of the Embalse del Pintado, you'll encounter the beautiful dehesa with The holm oaks approaching Santa María de Nava. The route also passes through the charming town of Cazalla de la Sierra, and along the scenic Río Benalija and Cuevas de Santiago Road.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: No, given its "difficult" classification, significant distance (over 111 km), and considerable elevation gain (over 1600 m), this route is not suitable for beginners or families with young children. It requires a very good level of fitness and experience in road cycling.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park offers a quieter, greener experience. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler with a chance of rain, so check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the Parque Natural Sierra Morena de Sevilla (which encompasses the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park). However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas and private land.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily a road cycling path, if you plan to bring a dog, they should be well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Always ensure your dog can handle the distance and terrain, and carry enough water for them.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this extensive route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll cross small sections of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, the Transandalus, and the Mtb Vía de la Plata, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through Cazalla de la Sierra, a town where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. Puebla de Maestre, your starting point, also offers amenities. However, it's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter while cycling?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park is known for its biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, including raptors. The dehesa environment supports a range of wildlife, so keep an eye out for local fauna, particularly in quieter sections of the route.
